channel.title: dotnet/sdk Issues
channel.description: Issues from https://github.com/dotnet/sdk, generated by https://github.com/meziantou/IssuesToRss/

item.title: Issue: Checksum mismatch upon downloading package- packages-microsoft-prod.deb on ubuntu 20.04 - @bitsdance
item.description:
Hello, All of a sudden the checksum of the package "packages-microsoft-prod.deb" is failing on ubuntu 20.04. We discounted the issued as we thought there might be mirror sync in the background, but It's been happening over 2 days now.

Environment: Distributor ID: Ubuntu Description: Ubuntu 20.04.1 LTS Release: 20.04 Codename: focal

Steps to reproduce - wget https://packages.microsoft.com/config/debian/10/packages-microsoft-prod.deb -O packages-microsoft-prod.deb dpkg -i packages-microsoft-prod.deb apt-get update -y

output:

wget https://packages.microsoft.com/config/debian/10/packages-microsoft-prod.deb -O packages-microsoft-prod.deb

--2021-07-21 12:42:44-- https://packages.microsoft.com/config/debian/10/packages-microsoft-prod.deb Resolving packages.microsoft.com (packages.microsoft.com)... 40.114.136.21 Connecting to packages.microsoft.com (packages.microsoft.com)|40.114.136.21|:443... connected. HTTP request sent, awaiting response... 200 OK Length: 3124 (3.1K) [application/octet-stream] Saving to: ‘packages-microsoft-prod.deb’

packages-microsoft-prod.deb 100%[===================================================================================================================>] 3.05K --.-KB/s in 0s

2021-07-21 12:42:44 (608 MB/s) - ‘packages-microsoft-prod.deb’ saved [3124/3124]

dpkg -i packages-microsoft-prod.deb (Reading database ... 31897 files and directories currently installed.) Preparing to unpack packages-microsoft-prod.deb ... Unpacking packages-microsoft-prod (1.0-debian10.1) over (1.0-debian10.1) ... Setting up packages-microsoft-prod (1.0-debian10.1) ..

apt-get update -y

E: Failed to fetch https://packages.microsoft.com/debian/10/prod/dists/buster/main/binary-armhf/Packages.bz2 File has unexpected size (14648 != 14446). Mirror sync in progress? [IP: 40.114.136.21 443] Hashes of expected file: - Filesize:14446 [weak] - SHA512:dcde6b0120201ffd3ce98ccc67a941c82206af4c9f6edacb21dececab859d151b6b05ab9f59c1c7a0215912eccb319a632c26580371f84dfb723f6cfb64ab807 - SHA256:8123690a1cfd7380c63554906b4bba25fc83142a3bc22c387ebc739f1427e1a5 - SHA1:f134ba1077765a3c3f1e84dc50f36cfb319eaa05 [weak] - MD5Sum:9dd887b5872fbb823fc0ba1aae7eb306 [weak] Release file created at: Tue, 20 Jul 2021 21:47:43 +0000 E: Some index files failed to download. They have been ignored, or old ones used instead.

item.title: Issue: <DefaultItemExcludes> does not work for xml files - @jonathanou
item.description:
I have a project with a folder named XML that contains .xml files.

I want to exclude the entire XML folder from the csproj. I'm trying to use DefaultItemExcludes but it's not working:

<PropertyGroup>
    <DefaultItemExcludes>$(DefaultItemExcludes);XML\**</DefaultItemExcludes>
</PropertyGroup>

However, the XML folder also contains a couple of python files, and they do get excluded from the csproj correctly (notice CodeGen.py is excluded in the second screenshot). For some reason .xml files are not excluded.

item.title: Issue: [API Compat] Investigate if we can remove dependency to Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Csharp - @safern
item.description:
We currently use this to load assemblies using CSharpCompilation as the language agnostic Compilation is an abstract class.

https://github.com/dotnet/sdk/blob/8d6b759abe5479eb46355dcd4bcfefe559b4fcbc/src/Compatibility/Microsoft.DotNet.ApiCompatibility/AssemblySymbolLoader.cs#L335

In order to remove this dependency we could explore writing our own agnostic compilation type that derives from Compilation and does what we need.

cc: @ericstj @Anipik

item.title: Issue: XmlStreamConfigurationProvider Support for external files via file="" or configSource="" - @Cephei
item.description:
Concerning Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration:

I've tried everything I can imagine to get this to work, but it seems the XmlConfigurationProvider does not support loading configuration from multiple xml files.

Example:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<configuration>
  <KESTREL configSource="ConfigurationFiles/kestrel.config" />
  <CONNECTIONSTRINGS configSource="ConfigurationFiles/connectionStrings.config" />
</configuration>

Can anyone confirm this is correct and that I would need to implement a custom configuration provider?

item.title: Issue: Running `dotnet workload install` sometimes installs newer workload versions with no way back - @danroth27
item.description:
If I run dotnet workload install for a specific workload it will first update the workload manifests to newer versions. This is sometimes problematic for me. For example, I installed a build of .NET 6 Preview 7 and then tried to install the WebAssembly build tools workload, which then ended up installing an RC1 version of the workload, which isn't what I wanted to test. I know that I can pass --skip-manifest-update, but the current experience feels like it's going to be a pit of failure. Once the workload manifest has been updated there is no clear way back if the newer version doesn't work for you. As far as I can tell, the best I can do is uninstall the workload and then manually delete the sdk-manifests folder and run a repair on my .NET SDK installation. I never want to manually mess around with the dotnet installation folder.

@KathleenDollard

item.title: Issue: Provide a way to list installed workloads and their versions - @danroth27
item.description: It looks like dotnet workload list shows you the list of available workloads to install, but how do you see which workloads are actually installed already? I'd also like to see the versions of the installed workloads.

item.title: Issue: How to make NET6 Android Binding NuGet usable on all API levels - @jpobst
item.description:
Let's say I want to create an Android Binding Library, I want it to target android-30 so I can access newer API, but be runnable on android-28 (and I will write runtime guards in my library):

<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net6.0-android</TargetFramework>  // Expands to 'net6.0-android30.0'
    <MinimumPlatformVersion>28.0</MinimumPlatformVersion>
    <SupportedOSPlatformVersion>28</SupportedOSPlatformVersion>
    <PackageId>My.Cool.Test.Package</PackageId>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>

When I publish this package on NuGet, it has a TargetFramework of net6.0-android30.0, and cannot be used by an application targeting net6.0-android28.0:

<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net6.0-android28.0</TargetFramework>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>

error NU1202: Package My.Cool.Test.Package 1.0.0 is not compatible with net6.0-android28.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v6.0). Package My.Cool.Test.Package 1.0.0 supports: net6.0-android30.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v6.0)
error NU1202: Package My.Cool.Test.Package 1.0.0 is not compatible with net6.0-android28.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v6.0) / android-arm64. Package My.Cool.Test.Package 1.0.0 supports: net6.0-android30.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v6.0)
error NU1202: Package My.Cool.Test.Package 1.0.0 is not compatible with net6.0-android28.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v6.0) / android-x86. Package My.Cool.Test.Package 1.0.0 supports: net6.0-android30.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v6.0)

This feels like a mismatch between dotnet using <TargetFramework> as a target, while NuGet uses it as a minimum.

This is an issue we are hitting trying to ensure our AndroidX/GooglePlayServices/etc packages are usable by people targeting versions of Android prior to current.

cc: @mhutch

item.title: Issue: Proxy with authentication doesn't work sometimes - @xky0007
item.description:
I met a very strange situation.

I'm running a dotnet core 3.1 console app in a linux server which behind corporate proxy. The proxy requires username and passwd authentication.

I set http_proxy, https_proxy, HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY to same value http://username:pw@server:port/ in case of case sensitive problems.

I have a loop to get an auth token from server every 20 mins. Since it's a small project and time interval is long, I use using(var client = new HttpClient() in the loop to call the server.

At the beginning, the client can pass the proxy and get the token. After an uncertain time of period, the client returned ProxyAuthenticationRequired status code. It will last some time and the error disappears later. The error may occur again after uncertain time.

Is there any method to print if the proxy has been used by the client? Therefore, I can do further investigation.

Thanks.

item.title: Issue: How do we check if workloads / workload packs are installed on first run experience? Do we have to go through the IAL, or just check files on disk? - @wli3
Issue: Add command line option to ``dotnet new sln`` that automatically adds all projects nested within it's folder or subfolders to solution. - @AraHaan
item.description:
Well for starters let's start with for example the roslyn repository. Pretend that for example Roslyn.sln does not exist and you wanted all 204+ projects to be in that solution file. It would be much nicer to just do dotnet new sln --add-projects and be done with it instead of doing dotnet add ... 204+ times just to add each project manually one by one.

@jmarolf also likes the idea as it would save time and besides 99.9999999999999999999% of codebases (and users) might actually want this to begin with.

If possible It probably could be an option to add to the dotnet new sln command in .NET 6 (perhaps with telemetry data that gets sent to provide usage details automatically).

item.title: Issue: .NET 6 Preview 7 breaks builds that set $(LangVersion) 8.0 - @jonathanpeppers
item.description:
Using 6.0.100-preview.7.21369.5, you can do:

dotnet new console
dotnet build -p:LangVersion=8.0 -bl

This fails with:

C:\src\globalusing\Program.cs(2,1): error CS8400: Feature 'top-level statements' is not available in C# 8.0. Please use language version 9.0 or greater. [C:\src\globalusing\globalusing.csproj]
C:\src\globalusing\obj\Debug\net6.0\globalusing.ImplicitNamespaceImports.cs(2,1): error CS8400: Feature 'global using directive' is not available in C# 8.0. Please use language version 10.0 or greater. [C:\src\globalusing\globalusing.csproj]
C:\src\globalusing\obj\Debug\net6.0\globalusing.ImplicitNamespaceImports.cs(3,1): error CS8400: Feature 'global using directive' is not available in C# 8.0. Please use language version 10.0 or greater. [C:\src\globalusing\globalusing.csproj]
C:\src\globalusing\obj\Debug\net6.0\globalusing.ImplicitNamespaceImports.cs(4,1): error CS8400: Feature 'global using directive' is not available in C# 8.0. Please use language version 10.0 or greater. [C:\src\globalusing\globalusing.csproj]
C:\src\globalusing\obj\Debug\net6.0\globalusing.ImplicitNamespaceImports.cs(5,1): error CS8400: Feature 'global using directive' is not available in C# 8.0. Please use language version 10.0 or greater. [C:\src\globalusing\globalusing.csproj]
C:\src\globalusing\obj\Debug\net6.0\globalusing.ImplicitNamespaceImports.cs(6,1): error CS8400: Feature 'global using directive' is not available in C# 8.0. Please use language version 10.0 or greater. [C:\src\globalusing\globalusing.csproj]
C:\src\globalusing\obj\Debug\net6.0\globalusing.ImplicitNamespaceImports.cs(7,1): error CS8400: Feature 'global using directive' is not available in C# 8.0. Please use language version 10.0 or greater. [C:\src\globalusing\globalusing.csproj]
C:\src\globalusing\obj\Debug\net6.0\globalusing.ImplicitNamespaceImports.cs(8,1): error CS8400: Feature 'global using directive' is not available in C# 8.0. Please use language version 10.0 or greater. [C:\src\globalusing\globalusing.csproj]

Binlog: msbuild.zip

item.title: Issue: dotnet publish, unable to target the framework 5.0.0 - @julienGrd
item.description:
Hello guys, i have a problem with the way my app is published. I wan't to target the framework 5.0.0 but the publish command use the version 5.0.6. I think the 5.0.6 version is installed on my computer because it was a part of the visual studio installation (but i also have the 5.0.0 installed). I see i can force the target framework with a global.json at the root of my project but it still doesn't work

{
  "sdk": {
    "version": "5.0.0" //i tried also 5.0.100
  }
}

there is my publish profile

<Project ToolsVersion="4.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <WebPublishMethod>FileSystem</WebPublishMethod>
    <LastUsedBuildConfiguration>Release</LastUsedBuildConfiguration>
    <LastUsedPlatform>Any CPU</LastUsedPlatform>
    <SiteUrlToLaunchAfterPublish />
    <LaunchSiteAfterPublish>True</LaunchSiteAfterPublish>
    <ExcludeApp_Data>False</ExcludeApp_Data>
    <ProjectGuid>a9a4c861-924a-4190-bc23-fd99cf48edd2</ProjectGuid>
    <publishUrl>bin\Release\net5.0-windows\publish</publishUrl>
    <DeleteExistingFiles>True</DeleteExistingFiles>
    <TargetFramework>net5.0-windows</TargetFramework>
    <RuntimeIdentifier>win-x86</RuntimeIdentifier>
    <SelfContained>true</SelfContained>
    <PublishTrimmed>True</PublishTrimmed>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>

I reference these framework : Microsoft.AspnetCore.App, Microsoft.NetCore.App, Microsoft.WindpwsDesktop.App.WindowsForms

i also reference these nuget packages

 <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore" Version="2.2.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.Abstractions" Version="2.2.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="5.0.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="Serilog.AspNetCore" Version="4.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>

the sdk installed on my machine

C:\WINDOWS\system32>dotnet --list-sdks
2.1.524 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
3.0.100 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
3.1.117 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
3.1.411 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
5.0.100-preview.8.20417.9 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
5.0.100-rc.1.20452.10 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
5.0.100-rc.2.20479.15 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
5.0.100 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
5.0.200-preview.20614.14 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]
5.0.400-preview.21328.4 [C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk]

My visual studio version is the 2019 Community Version 16.11.0 Preview 3.0

When i publish, i can see these line in the output (hundred of them)

-a "obj\Release\net5.0-windows\win-x86\Is.Geckos.LocalService.dll"
-a "C:\Users\julie\.nuget\packages\microsoft.windowsdesktop.app.runtime.win-x86\5.0.6\runtimes\win-x86\lib\net5.0\Accessibility.dll"
-a "C:\Users\julie\.nuget\packages\microsoft.windowsdesktop.app.runtime.win-x86\5.0.6\runtimes\win-x86\lib\net5.0\Microsoft.VisualBasic.Forms.dll"
...
-reference "C:\Users\julie\.nuget\packages\microsoft.aspnetcore.app.runtime.win-x86\5.0.6\runtimes\win-x86\lib\net5.0\Microsoft.AspNetCore.Connections.Abstractions.dll"

thanks for your help !

Julien

item.title: Issue: After installing .NET 6.0 Preview 7, the version information of ASP.NET Core Runtime is incorrectly displayed. - @v-sherryfan
item.description:
Repro Steps

  1. Download .NET 6 SDK(6.0.100-preview.7.21368.2) from Release/6.0.1XX-preview7 column in https://github.com/dotnet/installer
  2. Double click the .exe file go to install wizard.
  3. After the installation is successful, the version information of ASP.NET Core Runtime is incorrectly displayed.

Actual Result

item.title: Issue: Adjust sdk with global using - @wli3
item.description: Task name conflicts and <LangVersion>Preview</LangVersion> in directory.build.prop does not seem to work

item.title: Issue: [Daily SDK build] Microsoft.DotNet.MSBuildSdkResolver breaks VS2019 and VS2022 when trying to load any projects. - @AraHaan
item.description:
The thing used to work until about 5 days ago when I load it in Visual Studio. Ironically I can still load projects in Rider however Roslyn is too huge to load up into Rider and Rider sorta has issues with loading up Roslyn for me anyway.

I need this fixed as soon as possible as I need to make changes to Roslyn with the IDE, without it I cant get intelliscence to work to let me know if what code changes I make to roslyn actually will compile or not.

This not only breaks loading up Roslyn's projects but all of them on the machine they are installed on. And most of my projects depend on things that are in the daily builds of the SDKs.

Ironically it loads the projects file when I go about invoking dotnet build in command line.
